pi_items.source: Confirmed in export_result.sh (line 14) and task.json description (line 6)
All 4 bones are checked in verifier.py (line 69) and explicitly mentioned in task.json description line 3
All 4 mesh objects are created in setup_task.sh: LampBase (line 51), LampLowerArm (line 57), LampUpperArm (line 64), LampHead (line 71)
Verified against verifier.py lines 82-92 which check each parent-child relationship exactly as claimed

privileged_info_summary: The desk lamp armature rigging task uses synthetic data with four mesh objects (LampBase, LampLowerArm, LampUpperArm, LampHead) generated via Blender primitives in setup_task.sh. The task requires agents to create an armature with exactly four bones named Base, LowerArm, UpperArm, and Head, with a specific parent hierarchy: Base as root, LowerArm parented to Base, UpperArm parented to LowerArm, and Head parented to UpperArm. The output must be saved to /home/ga/BlenderProjects/lamp_rigged.blend. All mesh objects must be parented to the armature.
